title: _zstd: shared ZstdCompressor.flush() racing getattr('last_mode') is the same last_mode plain-write/member-atomic-read race as TSAN-0002 (duplicate)
description: Seeded from a fleet-02 _zstd vehicle labelled flush|flush. The captured race is ZstdCompressor.flush() writing self->last_mode as a plain C store under self->lock (compressor.c:679) vs getattr(c,'last_mode') reading that int lock-free with a relaxed-atomic load in PyMember_GetOne (structmember.c:64). It is NOT a distinct two-concurrent-flush cctx/stream-corruption bug: flush() holds self->lock around the entire compression context and both last_mode writes, so writer-vs-writer is fully serialized. Same field, same race face as TSAN-0002, reached via a different call shape (fleet-02's flush() + dir()-driven getattr sweep). Duplicate of TSAN-0002.
sites: Modules/_zstd/compressor.c _zstd_ZstdCompressor_flush_impl:679 (write side: plain store self->last_mode = mode, under self->lock)
Modules/_zstd/compressor.c _zstd_ZstdCompressor_flush_impl:682 (plain store self->last_mode = ZSTD_e_end, reset path, under self->lock)
Modules/_zstd/compressor.c _zstd_ZstdCompressor_compress_impl:631/634 (same plain writes, same race face)
Modules/_zstd/compressor.c ZstdCompressor_members:759 (last_mode published as read-only Py_T_INT member)
Python/structmember.c PyMember_GetOne:64 (read side: FT_ATOMIC_LOAD_INT_RELAXED of the last_mode member, no lock)
Modules/_zstd/compressor.c _zstd_ZstdCompressor_flush_impl:674/687 (PyMutex_Lock/Unlock: serializes all writers + cctx; does NOT cover the member-read path)
race_kind: plain write vs lock-free relaxed-atomic read (mutable int field exposed as Py_T_INT member; write side not converted to atomic). NOT a writer-vs-writer / cctx-corruption race: flush()/compress() serialize on self->lock.
shared_state: ZstdCompressor.last_mode (int; last compression end-directive). Writes guarded by self->PyMutex lock; member-descriptor read path (PyMember_GetOne) is lock-free. self->cctx and output buffers are fully lock-protected and do NOT race.
severity: low (value-benign, crash-free; aligned 4-byte int holding one of three enum constants; genuine TSan data race on read-only-looking getattr('last_mode') + flush()/compress(). The more serious concurrent-flush cctx/stream-corruption hypothesis is ruled out by the lock discipline and the control experiment.)
repro_reliability: reliable (exit 66 on every run, 3/3; SUMMARY compressor.c:679:25 in _zstd_ZstdCompressor_flush_impl; ~8 threads/compressor split flush-writers vs getattr('last_mode')-readers, fresh compressor per round behind a Barrier)
control_experiment: Pure flush|flush (8 threads calling only c.compress()+c.flush() on a shared compressor, no getattr) runs CLEAN: exit 0, zero TSan warnings -> confirms two concurrent flush() calls do NOT race on cctx/buffer/last_mode (serialized by self->lock).
suggested_fix: Convert the four last_mode stores (compressor.c:631,634,679,682) to FT_ATOMIC_STORE_INT_RELAXED to pair with the relaxed-atomic FT_ATOMIC_LOAD_INT_RELAXED already used by PyMember_GetOne; no new lock on the read path. One PR fixes both TSAN-0002 and TSAN-0017.
notes: The seeded flush|flush signature is a fleet-dedup artifact: the vehicle worker both calls flush() (headlines the SUMMARY as _zstd_ZstdCompressor_flush_impl) and reads getattr(c,'last_mode') via a dir(c) sweep, so both racing accesses trace to the same _zstd vehicle and the signature collapsed onto the flush frame. The real bug is the last_mode plain-write/member-atomic-read race = TSAN-0002. Modules/_zstd/ is new in 3.14, not on the gh-116738 audit list; last_mode stores still plain on current main. No separate upstream filing warranted; fold into the TSAN-0002 fix.
assessment: DUPLICATE of TSAN-0002 (same last_mode field; flush|flush was a dedup artifact -- control experiment with no getattr runs clean). Folded; signature moved to TSAN-0002. One PR fixes both.

related_issues.note: Modules/_zstd/ is NOT on the gh-116738 audit list (newer 3.14 module); last_mode stores still plain on current main.

related_findings.note: Same last_mode plain-write/PyMember_GetOne-atomic-read race; TSAN-0017 is a duplicate reached via a different call shape (fleet-02 op-mix).
